messages.text: Weight checks - 10:00am.
Shift 3 production start: OK
Maintenance has already been notified.

I am sending a video showing the moment the cards were passed through the metal detector; it clearly demonstrates that the yellow NFE card was rejected twice. The process worked correctly with both the blue and red cards. Maintenance has already been notified, and a verification is scheduled to take place during the next hour in their presence.
Corrected date and batch number
QA and maintenance did metal detector check no issues
<attachment id="ee7584b0-f398-4538-97fc-5e5b28bc5452"></attachment>
We are starting the changeover 30743-case 18
Product Presentation Change – Item # 30743 FEA MILK 60g DISPLAY BOX 10ct/CASE 18. Lot CC26133I1, Date 11/JAN/28 – Correct.
Started running 30743-case18
A metal detector check is performed; upon passing the blue S/S 316 card, it is rejected three times—along with the red and yellow cards. Is this correct?
MD and C6 belts running well

The metal detector check is being performed, and—as can be observed—it continues to reject items. Maintenance has been notified, but we are currently awaiting their assistance, as they are occupied with ML4. It is also reported that an electric current is present when removing the trays.
<attachment id="84ad18be-558a-499f-9b06-25801c512c60"></attachment>
Run of display now for 30767-case6
Beneath the C6 belt—specifically inside a tray—we found a piece of broken belt. I have attached a video demonstrating that the belt is splitting apart.
Maintenance is already being called.

Sending a video of the metal detector check, which demonstrates that the yellow and blue cards are rejected multiple times. Maintenance has already been notified, but they are currently occupied at ML4.
Having issues with C6 belt it's started to tear in the northside
<attachment id="5d1444a5-5a18-4c77-bf42-cdc6c4796ada"></attachment>
We have an EE watching it for now .when we go down for next changeover Maintenance can check it again

Thank you for the video. 

Chris Givens,  is there any ideas on this one?

We have only seen this type of reaction on S3 which is a different type of metal detector and set up.
Midshift
C6 belt is now running in a better position and no longer riding on the edge.
The metal detector was checked, but it continues to reject all cards; maintenance inspected the unit, yet the issue persists. Furthermore, power continues to flow even when the trays are removed.
Thank you Janeth!
The order had already been completed during the night shift, but the cooler is still full—and the PTL is too. Let's empty the cooler.
The machine is set to melting mode.
Countdown
5/13/26 Broken sensor count:
This is from the inclusion hopper.
The c6 belt is fixed
ATP ✅ passed The Rice Pot Stage

The temper is high. Should we use the milk recipe again to run Crunch? The last time we ran Crunch, we did it that way, or should we continue making adjustments to the mixing water to see if it improves? Robert Villarreal Rich Brierley
First set everything to CL, except put water jacket at 94.0
Robert, could you verify that we have two different centerlines for Crunch in the system? I already printed the ones you just sent us. Robert Villarreal

Fixed!
I just noticed this as well. I removed the old one
The tempering for Crunch is not updated like the one for Plain. Could you please verify that as well?
Robert Villarreal
The machine was moved to the centerlines, and—following Robert's instructions—the water jacket was set to 94.
Maintenance is working on the rice hammer mill.

Grey,
Temper sheet updated. Thank you
The chocolate is 10175, and I can only find the one for 10262.
I just put it in there now
Maintenance has now fixed the hopper vacuum problem, as it was not suctioning the rice.
The rice hopper vacuum is now working. After doing a tear down we discovered the flap was bent causing a vacuum leak.
Adjust mixing water please Imperial
We do it now
The percentage of chocolate and rice falls within the range.
I will be there in 5 minutes Imperial

This cart by Theegarten has covers from the belt, please do not move it, as tomorrow we will continue the environmental clean with 1st shift crew.
Justin Garner Dakota Adair Jody Howse

Curt Willis Justin Garner Tiffanee Taylor Junior Quinonez Curt Willis Chad Stone  there was a lot of work done to get the tarp sealed to the floor  on S1 yesterday Jovani from 2nd shift lindon 2 warehouse used our forklift yesterday after 6:30pm  and didn't bother to tell anyone that he pulled the tarp up and took the fork lift from line one construction to Lindon 2 I'm assuming, he text me if he could use it I told him to go out the roll up door we installed and not to go through production I gave him the lock code as well to open the door.. We taped the curtain back to the floor already.
There’s a hole in tent needs immediate attention
This corner
Miriah Manzanares Curt Willis, Chad Stone Marco Rodriguez Justin Garner
It is really hard to get to and see from this corner I had to hold my phone down to get the picture
Do we know how long the hole has been there?
QA will check hourly and post a picture.
Your good we're getting it done right now
Thanks we are sealing it from the inside  and I'm going around to the production side
Midshift 1st shift

Premium Caramel testing is being conducted





Viscosity, WA, Brix
Viscosity,WA,Brix

Mariela Gudino let’s look into a different spindle. 
I’m going to look at the manual and see which one will make it so we don’t reduce RPMs that much.
Mariela Gudino Can yous ee if we have a spindle 03?
This will allow us to keep RPM consistent between 5-10.

Transition to Brookfield standard geometry (Spindle #4 with 600 mL beaker) produced significantly higher and more stable viscosity readings; current torque levels (~88%) indicate spindle size may be too large for target operating RPM, and evaluation of smaller spindle (#3) is recommended for method optimization

Hi Team, update: it was only necessary to cook 2 batches to obtain a good result for the Classic Caramel 20001. We left 5 trays in the cooler to check the texture tomorrow. Volker travels back to Germany tomorrow, and I will be taking a few days of vacation. See you next week.
ATP (0) Allergen Pass Flow wrap S4

Chad Stone, Curt Willis, Justin Garner, Matt Hatch, Daniel Bringas, Ira Robison, and Marcos Jimenez
 
The current plan for the S4 line is to commission using Classic Caramel first, followed by a 4-week Bequet ramp-up.
 
However, I am receiving conflicting information between the production schedule and Sales regarding the needed Bequet production volume. We also currently only have one pallet of twist wrap film requested for commissioning. Additionally, Ira mentioned the customer may not want that much sellable product due to shelf life concerns.
 
Ira is meeting with Bequet today, and afterward I will schedule a meeting so we can align on the commissioning and ramp-up plan.

Jay Alba, I just forwarded you a quote from Chocotech who is requesting a PO ASAP since Germany has a holiday tomorrow and they will be closed for the rest of the week. They need the PO so Volker can schedule travel and be onsite next week to support the S4 commissioning/startup.
 
Could you please help create the PO by 12 PM today? 
 
Thanks and apologies for the short notice.

2 broken sensors for Cavanna 1
We found two broken sensors on the Cavanna 1—the B142 and the B141; these are the speed sensors. I have shut down the machine and have already called Maintenance.
Chad Stone Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Alan Greer Marcos Montano

Two identical labels were found on different pallets of chocolate; moreover, they did not correspond to the product currently being used on the line. We handed them over to IMS so that they could handle the matter.
Weight final piece in the range
Cavanna 1 is now back in operation after replacing the sensors.
Date  and lot  is correct  the sealing is approopriate
The sensors have already been replaced as part of routine maintenance, and Cavanna 1 is now running smoothly.
The pallet of salt located on the line bears a label with item number 10001 and a label with item number 10002; the correct label is the one with item number 10002.
Good morning, we reviewed and corrected, thank you
In order to prevent the buffer tank from overflowing, these 2  valves must be closed manually

Hello! Just a heads up, the supply count has been completed & we need the following ordered asap. 

Metal detectable pens, safety glasses, shoe covers, Peco pallets, machine box tape (S3 only version), & V-904Q ink. 

Can we make sure to order enough of the Peco pallets  to get us back to the standardized minimum required QTY for these please? We have about 370 Peco pallets available & the standardized minimum is 1,620. 

We have been low over the past few weeks & it would help production a ton to have pallets in reserve just in case. 

Junior Quinonez-Curt Willis, Sunny Jagtiani-Ethan Bean-Tiffanee Taylor-Chad Stone-Jennifer Loveless

Sunny Jagtiani,

I know we have been talking PECO pallets. 

Is this something that can be done soon? (1620 minimum)

Is 1620 the right number after all the added trailers, etc?

Curt Willis Chad Stone Jody Howse
